output_summary: aligned schemas for datasets: discogs, lastfm, musicbrainz; generated 2 schema correspondence groups.
profiled datasets: discogs, lastfm, musicbrainz.
Reused the blocking configuration from state.
Blocking uses `semantic_similarity` for discogs_lastfm, musicbrainz_lastfm, blocking on name, artist; `sorted_neighbourhood` for discogs_musicbrainz, blocking on name.
Performance-wise, pair completeness ranges from 0.90 to 0.96 and candidate counts range from 47615 to 225831.
The config also records dataset id columns.

matcher mode `rule_based` with config source reused from state; evaluated pairs: id_columns, matching_strategies; selected scores: matching_strategies (threshold=0.6, f1=0.896551724137931, threshold=0.7, f1=0.8732394366197184, threshold=0.75); produced 2 pair strategy entries.
This run was an initial draft of the pipeline.
The pipeline was created to load discogs, lastfm, and musicbrainz from CSV files and align schemas by renaming lastfm and musicbrainz columns to the discogs schema using an LLM-based schema matcher.
The matching stage was defined with pair-specific blocking and rule-based comparators for all three dataset pairs: discogs-lastfm, discogs-musicbrainz, and musicbrainz-lastfm.

The fusion stage was added with a rule-based fusion strategy that applies longest_string to seven scalar attributes and union to three track-list attributes, then writes fused output and fusion debug logs.
Fusion behavior is centered on longest_string as the dominant resolver for textual and scalar fields, while collection-like track attributes are merged with union.

Matching behavior shifted to pair-specific configurations instead of a single global setup, using embedding blocking for discogs-lastfm and musicbrainz-lastfm, and sorted neighbourhood blocking for discogs-musicbrainz.
Schema alignment was introduced before entity matching so downstream matching and fusion operate on a shared discogs-oriented column schema.

pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 2; fusion_data.csv was produced.
This run produced an initial draft of the evaluation adaptation script.
The evaluation used a rule-based, field-specific strategy after first building an eval_id-aligned view of fused records against the gold XML test set.
The script aligned fused rows to gold identifiers through parsed _fusion_sources, gold-prefix detection, and confidence-based deduplication before running DataFusionEvaluator.
Name, artist, release-country, label, and genre were evaluated with tokenized_match.
Release-date was evaluated with year_only_match.
Duration was evaluated with numeric_tolerance_match.
Track-level fields such as tracks_track_name, tracks_track_position, and tracks_track_duration were evaluated with set_equality_match.
Any additional detected list-like non-system columns were normalized and then evaluated with set_equality_match.
The script added _parse_source_ids to safely parse source identifier collections from JSON-like or literal string representations.
The script added _detect_gold_prefix to infer the dominant gold ID prefix and use it when mapping fused source IDs to gold records.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 55.84%; evaluation attempt counter is 1.
The main problem in this run is fusion-level bad value selection within matched clusters rather than blocking or matcher threshold tuning.
The current fusion configuration is choosing incorrect scalar values and incoherent track data, and it is also omitting some gold records from fused output coverage.
Overall accuracy is 0.5583756345177665 with macro accuracy 0.5617753623188405 across 197 evaluations and 23 evaluated records.
The weakest attribute accuracies are tracks_track_name at 0.08695652173913043, tracks_track_duration at 0.2, and duration at 0.391304347826087.
Examples of incorrect fused values include choosing "Guns At Dawn / Ratpack" instead of "Guns At Dawn" and "The Outcasts - The Punk Singles Collection" instead of "The Punk Singles Collection".
The analysis reports repeated missing_fused_value events for gold ids mbrainz_25432, mbrainz_3787, and mbrainz_29595.
Track positions often contain too many tracks, which is consistent with naive union across inconsistent source records.
The pipeline uses longest_string for name, artist, release-date, release-country, duration, label, and genre.
The pipeline uses union for tracks_track_name, tracks_track_position, and tracks_track_duration.
This run was a refinement of the evaluated pipeline rather than an initial draft or repair.

The fusion strategy was rewritten from generic `longest_string` and `union` rules to attribute-specific custom fusers such as `choose_name`, `choose_artist`, `choose_release_date`, `choose_release_country`, `choose_duration`, `choose_label`, and `choose_genre`.

Track attribute fusers for `tracks_track_name`, `tracks_track_position`, and `tracks_track_duration` were changed from `union` to placeholder lambdas returning `None`, with tracklist selection logic implemented outside the direct per-column fusion rules.
Singleton retention was enabled by changing `include_singletons` from `False` to `True`.
Post-fusion support code was added for coherent tracklist selection using `choose_best_track_record` and scoring based on track completeness and source priority.
The pipeline shifted away from length-based scalar conflict resolution toward normalized majority-style and type-aware fusion for names, artists, dates, countries, durations, labels, and genres.
The handling of track metadata shifted away from column-wise union across sources toward selecting a single best source tracklist as a coherent record-level object.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.
This run was an evaluation refinement rather than an initial draft or repair.

The evaluation script was adjusted slightly, including renaming the strategy, removing the explicit genre evaluator registration, and reordering one registration call without changing the core evaluation approach.
The dominant strategy in this run was to refine evaluation around fusion-specific failure diagnosis rather than retune blocking or matching.
The analysis treated poor fused value selection as the primary issue and used attribute-specific evaluation functions to inspect scalar, date, numeric, and list-like fields.
The run evaluated fused records through an eval-id expansion view built from fusion source provenance so gold musicbrainz identifiers could be aligned to fused clusters.

Scalar text fields such as name, artist, release-country, and label were evaluated mainly with tokenized matching and were analyzed as needing normalized majority-vote or source-aware fusion instead of longest-string selection.

Temporal and numeric fields such as release-date and duration were treated separately, using year-only comparison for dates and numeric tolerance for duration, with the analysis recommending parsed and type-aware fusion for both.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 59.39%; previous logged accuracy was 55.84%; evaluation attempt counter is 2.
The run is underperforming primarily because the fusion layer is producing wrong or missing fused attribute values rather than because of blocking failures.
The weakest errors are concentrated in fusion-heavy fields, especially release-country, track names, track durations, and album duration.
There is also an evaluation-facing normalization mismatch for release-country, where semantically equivalent short forms are emitted instead of the long-form labels used in gold.
Some clusters also appear contaminated or over-merged, causing implausible artist, label, and country selections inside fusion.
Overall accuracy is 0.5939086294416244 and macro accuracy is 0.5977657004830917 across 197 evaluations.

The lowest attribute accuracies are release-country_accuracy = 0.30434782608695654, tracks_track_duration_accuracy = 0.35, tracks_track_name_accuracy = 0.391304347826087, and duration_accuracy = 0.43478260869565216.

Higher accuracies on artist_accuracy = 0.8260869565217391, name_accuracy = 0.8260869565217391, and label_accuracy = 0.8125 indicate the worst degradation is concentrated in specific fused fields rather than uniformly across all attributes.
This run was a refinement in evaluation_refinement mode.
The pipeline replaced null-returning fusers for tracks_track_name, tracks_track_position, and tracks_track_duration with choose_label-based attribute fusers.
The fusion code added broader normalization and voting utilities, including normalize_text_key, choose_best_original_for_key, weighted_vote_original, and expanded duration and country handling.
The pipeline introduced source-specific weighting tables and source_weight logic to support attribute-aware trust weighting across musicbrainz, discogs, and lastfm.
Fusion shifted away from explicitly disabling track attributes toward directly fusing track fields through configured attribute fusers.

Fusion shifted from short-string-biased tie breaking toward selecting richer original strings through normalized key grouping and longest-canonical selection for name, artist, label, and release-country.

Country output shifted from short canonical forms such as United States and United Kingdom to long-form evaluation-compatible canonical labels such as United States of America and United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland.

pipeline execution failed on attempt 1: error: SyntaxError: Non-UTF-8 code starting with '\x91' in file D:\Studium\Master\Semester 5\Team Project\workspace\data-integration-team-project\agents\output\code\pipeline.py on line 78, but no encoding declared; see https://peps.python.o; fusion_data.csv was produced.
This run was a repair iteration executed in execution_repair mode after a previous pipeline failure caused by a non-UTF-8 syntax error.

The pipeline was reworked to introduce weighted source-aware fusion helpers such as weighted_choose_name, weighted_choose_artist, weighted_choose_release_country, weighted_choose_release_date, weighted_choose_duration, weighted_choose_label, and weighted_choose_genre.

The normalization logic was expanded for text, artist, and country handling, including long-form canonical country mapping for United States of America and United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland variants.
A coherence filter was added through choose_most_coherent_rows to favor rows agreeing on core name and artist keys before fusion decisions.
Duration handling was strengthened with weighted_choose_duration, which groups close values, ignores nonpositive durations, and can reconcile album duration with summed fused track durations.

The intended fusion behavior shifted from simple unweighted attribute voting and earlier brittle tie-breaking toward weighted, source-aware selection with MusicBrainz, Discogs, and Last.fm attribute-specific trust scores.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 2; fusion_data.csv was produced.
This run was an evaluation refinement rather than an initial draft or a repair.

The dominant strategy was to refine evaluation around fusion failure modes by prioritizing changes that better measure and expose attribute-selection errors, null propagation, and normalization mismatches.
The run also adjusted evaluation execution details such as eval-id mapping, list-like normalization, and strategy selection so the assessment would align more reliably with the fused output.
Track-list fields were grouped together as the weakest fusion family, with track name, track duration, and track position treated as needing aligned list fusion instead of single-record selection.

Country fields were treated as a normalization-sensitive group, with release-country singled out for evaluation-compatible canonical output because semantically equivalent short and long forms were being scored as wrong.

Core scalar identity fields such as name, artist, and label were grouped under scalar fusion tie-breaking, where weak shortest-string selection was identified as a source of avoidable wrong values in noisy clusters.
